programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny dig deeper into the lives of two of the people mentioned in the diary. They begin with Father Robert Greaves, who remembers Jimmy fondly, but is unable to help identify the mysterious 'Jo-Jo', the person who it appears gave Jimmy the journal. The detectives also meet Eric Slater, the former bookkeeper to Arlingham House. He also remembers the victim well - but can he provide any clues to his disappearance? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Bernard Hill and Tom Courtenay.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: As the investigation into the cold-case murder continues, the forensic team discovers that the marks on the victim's body are consistent with known gangland torture methods. With this in mind, Cassie and Sunny question Sir Phillip about his involvement with a prominent East End crime family 40 years ago. Did the businessman or any of the shady associates of his youth have anything to do with Jimmy's disappearance or murder? Meanwhile, Eric and Claire's anniversary gets under way. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Trevor Eve and Tom Courtenay.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The villagers fear for a boy's life when he disappears while exploring the gardens of a derelict house with his brother and sister. Wetherby learns he has fallen down an old well and a media circus descends on the scene as firemen begin their rescue attempt. However, panic ensues when shots are fired from the house. Meanwhile, a troublesome celebrity journalist goes in search of local gossip.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Oh! To Be in England - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby imagines he is rich after being left a sizeable sum in his uncle's will. But there is a condition attached - and the landlord has to seek Miss Jones's help if he has any hope of seeing the money. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.desc: Biff assures Dave that he's not going to take advantage of Linda. The village disco ends in embarrassment for Butch. Biff catches Dave and Kim together.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers worry when Linda doesn't come home. Chris and Rachel's wedding day arrives. Frank takes some drastic measures and disowns Chris.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia keeps Jamie off school to guard the house. Cliff's solicitor asks Jack for a meeting. Tracy rows with Deirdre for cutting her off.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Denise wishes Deirdre would move away so she and Ken can get on with their lives. Josie is attacked while locking up the surgery. The Duckworths receive some stunning news.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Cheap Flat
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse is assigned to protect an informant who is undergoing emergency surgery. The case brings back unpleasant memories for the detective, while his colleagues are thrown into disarray when Bright is also rushed to hospital. Morse becomes fixated with a series of mysterious deaths that have occurred on the ward, and tries to find the scientific explanation behind stories of a curse.(n)

programme.title: Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: When recently widowed Vivien announces her love for a new man, Mark, it throws her family of adult children into chaos. The siblings' suspicions about Mark are fuelled by rising tensions in their own lives. Helen's focus on her job has led to a rift in her marriage, Jake's gambling debts have caused his wife and kids to leave him, and Natalie is lying to everybody about her secret relationship. Meanwhile, Vivien's neighbour, Mary, is simply trying to protect the family she has known for years. But in a few weeks one of these people will be lying on the beach, terribly injured - perhaps dead. Drama, starring Francesca Annis, Imelda Staunton and Stephen Rea.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The father of a missing schoolgirl demands action, angry that a six-month investigation has failed to uncover any leads. Morse ends up being assigned the case - and discovers some unpleasant truths about her family and teachers during the course of his inquiries. John Thaw stars as the Oxford detective, with Kevin Whately, Peter Woodthorpe, Peter McEnery, Elizabeth Hurley and Frances Tomelty.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby decides to express repentance for his numerous sins, at the urging of his latest lodger, a religious student. Classic comedy, starring Leonard Rossiter, Don Warrington and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head for rural Cornwall, where they create a spectacular contemporary garden for Chris Finney, a former serviceman who was awarded the George Cross in 2003. A space age-inspired area - complete with solar dome - will test Chris's knowledge as a novice gardener, and provide a stimulating haven for his young family.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Chessington to create a car-themed garden for hospital worker and automobile fan Alan Rendall. In July 2014, he managed to rescue his unconscious neighbour, Roy Whiteway, from his burning house, but was unable to save Dorothy, Roy's wife, as the fire was so intense. To recognise his bravery, Alan and the team surprise the whole Rendall family and turn their featureless plot into an automobile-inspired haven.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen receives a shocking diagnosis from Dr Gaddas, leading Geoff to immediately accuse her of infidelity, while Dev makes matters worse for Asha, who begs him to withdraw his complaints. Gary warns Claire that if Rick finds out she has been pocketing his cash, he is likely to kill her.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff checks the secret security camera feed on his phone and is intrigued to see Yasmeen stuffing clothes into a holdall and locking it in a cupboard by the stairs. When Summer explains to Billy that Kelly's mum has abandoned her, he agrees to let her stay at their flat, while Dev agrees to phone the school and try to withdraw his complaint.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Matters come to a head between Geoff and Yasmeen after another traumatic series of events, which begin with Geoff locking his long-suffering partner in the house having first confiscated her phone.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth has retired to a charming English village in the hope of finding peace and quiet. However, the murder of a rich industrialist neighbour forces him to leave his new lifestyle behind as he plunges into a sordid world of blackmail and envy, aided by the dogged Chief Inspector Japp. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Malcolm Terris and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: The Queen (2006)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning fact-based drama, examining the events surrounding the death of Princess Diana. The relationship between the monarch and Prime Minister Tony Blair is revealed as they struggle to reach a compromise in dealing with the situation. Starring Helen Mirren in an Oscar-winning role, alongside Michael Sheen, James Cromwell, Helen McCrory and Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell.(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective investigates the disappearance of a Dutch au pair, which seems connected to rivalry amid the city's academics. His search leads him to a family estate in the Oxfordshire countryside where he meets three siblings divided by personal demons and shared loss. Endeavour suspects there is a link between the disappearance and an unsolved case from years before, but as the investigation continues, conflict erupts between his fellow officers. When a group of teenagers makes a horrifying discovery, he suspects someone is manipulating events from afar.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: ITV3 Most Wanted - Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The 'Most Wanted' episode of Agatha Christie's Poirot, starring David Suchet as the titular Belgian sleuth, who makes innovative use of lateral thinking and his 'little grey cells' to solve various crimes. Will one of the earlier entries in the canon make it to the top of the list, or one of those regarded as all-time classics of the detective genre?(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The local gossips go to town when a naked and mutilated corpse is discovered in the middle of a crop circle. However, their lurid tales of extraterrestrial abduction soon take a turn for the macabre when more similarly injured cadavers are discovered, causing DCI Barnaby to question his down-to-earth approach. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An American tourist's body is discovered in her hotel room, apparently dead from a heart attack - but a valuable Anglo-Saxon jewel she intended to donate to the Ashmolean Museum is missing and several interested parties are acting strangely. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Simon Callow, Roberta Taylor and Kenneth Cranham.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Nearly 40 years after he left her mother to return to Nigeria, mum-of-three Laura searches for her birth father, who promised that he would come back for her. Meanwhile, 71-year-old Linda from Somerset searches for her twin sisters, who were given up for adoption by their father when her mother sadly passed away and he could no longer cope with Linda and her four younger siblings on his own.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: In a bid to find true love, Rigsby puts a personal advertisement in the local paper and receives an unexpected reply. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour and Don Warrington star.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen receives a shocking diagnosis from Dr Gaddas, leading Geoff to immediately accuse her of infidelity, while Dev makes matters worse for Asha, who begs him to withdraw his complaints. programme.desc: Matters come to a head between Geoff and Yasmeen after another traumatic series of events, which begin with Geoff locking his long-suffering partner in the house having first confiscated her phone.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is shocked when the foster parents arrive and accuse Will of threatening them, while Jamie secretly plans to meet up with Belle, and Vanessa asks Rhona to be Johnny's guardian in the event of her death.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle implores Jamie to forget about their affair and fix things with Andrea, while DI Malone explains a new dangerous job to Cain, Will and Billy - who cannot help but feel apprehensive. Leanna is pleased to cause a rift between Liam and Leyla, and Paddy struggles to hide his anxiety.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is startled to see the scale of the drug order he is being told to courier and even more shocked when he realises Billy and Cain are being used as decoys to draw police attention away from him. Chas is shocked at how much Paddy has been struggling with Eve, and Andrea struggles to keep a cool demeanour in front of Jamie and Millie.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Loving (1970)
programme.desc: A couple who run a dating agency find themselves inundated with customers, and end up arranging a selection of very unlikely matches - while also contending with their own marital difficulties. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Terry Scott.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny dig deeper into the lives of two of the people mentioned in the diary. They begin with Father Robert Greaves, who remembers Jimmy fondly, but is unable to help identify the mysterious 'Jo-Jo', the person who it appears gave Jimmy the journal. The detectives also meet Eric Slater, the former bookkeeper to Arlingham House. He also remembers the victim well - but can he provide any clues to his disappearance? Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Bernard Hill and Tom Courtenay.(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: As the investigation into the cold-case murder continues, the forensic team discovers that the marks on the victim's body are consistent with known gangland torture methods. With this in mind, Cassie and Sunny question Sir Phillip about his involvement with a prominent East End crime family 40 years ago. Did the businessman or any of the shady associates of his youth have anything to do with Jimmy's disappearance or murder? Meanwhile, Eric and Claire's anniversary gets under way. Crime drama, starring Nicola Walker, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Trevor Eve and Tom Courtenay.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The presenter continues his epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter. On the third and final leg of his odyssey, he begins in Northern Canada before travelling into the icy wilderness of Alaska. Along the way, Alexander meets people who have headed north to discover adventure and seek their fortune, as well as digging for gold with modern day prospectors. He also takes on the challenge of driving up one of the deadliest roads in the world, before concluding his travels on the international dateline between America and Russia.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The Leopard's Den residents are devastated when Du Plessis reveals he is moving to England to be with Caroline - especially Danny when he realises his friend's flight leaves the following day. Fatani buys a lion that will not come out of its cage, and when Rosie investigates she discovers a shocking case of negligence by the previous owner, so vows to do whatever is necessary to save the man's other animals. Deon Stewardson, Lucy-Jo Hudson and Stephen Tompkinson star.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is shocked when the foster parents arrive and accuse Will of threatening them, while Jamie secretly plans to meet up with Belle, and Vanessa asks Rhona to be Johnny's guardian in the event of her death.(n)

programme.title: World of Sport: Kings of the Canvas
programme.desc: Richard Henwood takes a look back at the glory days of British wrestling with some classic action from ITV's sport archives.(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A mentally unbalanced criminal sets out in search of vengeance for the death of his brother and frames Harry for murder. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Featuring a family farm making jam for the Queen's breakfast table, a Sheffield man producing classic safety razors, a bed-maker using recycled bottles to make mattresses, and a British clockmaker paying tribute to a man that changed the course of history. Ricky Tomlinson narrates.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A profile of a family making traditional haggis, revealing the dish may not have come from Scotland after all. Plus, a look at what goes into making a set of bowls fit for a champion, how a firm makes the wheelbarrows that helped rebuild Britain, and a team making classic brogues in demand worldwide.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to the rivers of India's Himalayan region to investigate a fresh spate of attacks by a type of catfish, but his plans to catch a specimen run into trouble when locals reveal it is sacred and he will be cursed if it is touched. As the hunt progresses, Wade's single-minded determination leads him into a psychological battle with the creature.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by guest angler Gary Newman as they head to Croatia, where they target a rare 30lb grass carp. They also venture onto a wild river that will test their skills to the limit as they look to land a huge king carp.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney visit three diverse venues across England. They first target barbel on the River Trent alongside Matt Godfrey, before switching their attention to carp on the Grand Union Canal, and finally teaming up with Tom Dove and Steve Ringer at Earlswood Lake.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Bold Contender and Skipper Squizzy head south to Port Davey for one last roll of the dice, but Glenn and the crew of Ansons Bay are also fishing in the same area.(n)

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee.(n)

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest entertainment news.(n)

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee.(n)

programme.title: Battle of Britain (1969)
programme.desc: In 1940, the Luftwaffe launches a campaign of aerial attacks against Britain to gain control of the skies ahead of a planned land invasion. Under the leadership of Air Chief Marshal Hugh Dowding, the RAF mounts a determined defence over the summer and autumn, despite being heavily outnumbered and forced to match inexperienced pilots against German veterans. Second World War drama, starring Laurence Olivier, Michael Caine, Robert Shaw, Christopher Plummer, Harry Andrews, Trevor Howard and Curt Jurgens.(n)

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. Over the years, the family experiences the key events of the nation's history, including the gold rush, the Civil War, and the building of the railroads. Epic Western, starring James Stewart, John Wayne, Gregory Peck, Henry Fonda, Debbie Reynolds, Lee J Cobb and Richard Widmark.(n)

programme.title: Nevada Smith (1966)
programme.desc: The son of a Native American mother and a white father dedicates his life to gaining revenge on the three outlaws who murdered them. Western adventure, starring Steve McQueen, Karl Malden, Brian Keith, Arthur Kennedy and Suzanne Pleshette.(n)

programme.title: In the Line of Fire (1993)
programme.desc: An ageing secret service agent, tormented by his failure to save the life of John F Kennedy in 1963, is drawn into a battle of wits with a sinister CIA-trained assassin out to kill the current president. As the day of reckoning draws closer, the agent's nerves are tested to the limit - and nothing seems to be going his way. Wolfgang Petersen's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, John Malkovich, Rene Russo and Dylan McDermott.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A doctor tries to lay claim to the body of a youth found dead on a building site to aid in his research, but Quincy suspects he is trying to cover up a murder.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.sub-title: A Shield for Murder - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Theo tries to prove that an imprisoned woman is innocent, but is framed for intimidating an informant to stop him getting to the truth. Geraldine Page guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An ailing diplomat asks Holmes to retrieve a document about a highly sensitive treaty between Britain and Italy, which an opportunistic thief has stolen from his desk at the Foreign Office. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and David Gwillim.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.sub-title: Arthur Is Dead, Long Live Arthur
programme.desc: Arthur must find a way of paying a £20,000 tax bill immediately, but fortunately Dave has a drastic solution - faking a drowning accident. Chisholm is suspicious as there is no body, while Arthur is spotted hiding out in a hotel. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates a consortium building a lethal fighter plane in eastern Europe, that appears willing to kill to supress publication of the aircraft's appalling safetly record. Lynda Bellingham guest stars, with Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter stake out a dodgy garage, believed to be the base of a criminal gang. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star in the first episode of the crime-busting drama, with Ian Hendry, Brian Blessed and June Brown.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The author of a best-selling diet book is taken to task by Quincy, who suspects that his dubious advice on healthy eating has led to the death of a model at the peak of her career.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo helps a Yugoslavian princess who has come to New York disguised as a nun to recover her stolen family jewels - now in the hands of a mobster. Maria Schell guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A music teacher asks Holmes to discover the identity of a mysterious bearded cyclist who often follows at a distance as she makes her way to work. Period crime drama, guest starring Barbara Wilshere, Michael Siberry and John Castle.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur unwittingly causes a diplomatic incident by trying to strike a deal with the crew of a visiting Russian ship. Guest starring Rula Lenska, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are trained to act as bodyguards to a Middle Eastern president who is preparing to sign an important peace treaty. Meanwhile, two killers are receiving similar training to prepare them to assassinate him. As their paths cross, the CI5 operatives learn the would-be killers have some useful inside information and share more in common with them than they may have imagined.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Members of Regan's own flying squad fall under suspicion when £35,000 in cash goes missing during an operation to protect a security van. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney travel to Dorset with England international angler Matt Godfrey, where they target a 20lb carp on an overnight challenge, before trying their hand at sea fishing for wrasse at Portland Bill, and finally trying to catch a 5lb chub at Throop Fishery.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Three boats - the Triton, the Bold Contender and Ansons Bay - continue to seach for lobsters with the winter season closing in two days' time.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Featuring a family farm making jam for the Queen's breakfast table, a Sheffield man producing classic safety razors, a bed-maker using recycled bottles to make mattresses, and a British clockmaker paying tribute to a man that changed the course of history. Ricky Tomlinson narrates.(n)

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: A profile of a family making traditional haggis, revealing the dish may not have come from Scotland after all. Plus, a look at what goes into making a set of bowls fit for a champion, how a firm makes the wheelbarrows that helped rebuild Britain, and a team making classic brogues in demand worldwide.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade heads to Russia's remote Amur River, where the mysterious deaths of three men appear to be linked to a freshwater giant - a beast that can exceed 18ft in length and weigh more than a ton. However, he encounters a wall of silence and begins to suspect he has stumbled on to a dark secret that isn't supposed to be uncovered.(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade heads to the rivers of India's Himalayan region to investigate a fresh spate of attacks by a type of catfish, but his plans to catch a specimen run into trouble when locals reveal it is sacred and he will be cursed if it is touched. As the hunt progresses, Wade's single-minded determination leads him into a psychological battle with the creature.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by guest angler Gary Newman as they head to Croatia, where they target a rare 30lb grass carp. They also venture onto a wild river that will test their skills to the limit as they look to land a huge king carp.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney visit three diverse venues across England. They first target barbel on the River Trent alongside Matt Godfrey, before switching their attention to carp on the Grand Union Canal, and finally teaming up with Tom Dove and Steve Ringer at Earlswood Lake.(n)

programme.desc: Sheldon bids an emotional goodbye to Fun with Flags as he and Amy record the final instalment of the internet show. Leonard, Howard and Raj clear out the office of a recently deceased professor and take a peek at his research. Guest starring LeVar Burton.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is forced to eat bland food after he burns his taste buds while trying to copy a fire-eating street performer. He becomes a regular at Springfield Elementary's canteen where he learns a new parenting technique to try out on Bart and Lisa - but its overbearing nature leads to disaster. Meanwhile, Marge enjoys a sauna she discovers in the basement.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer rent an apartment in an affluent area so their children can have a good education when Springfield Elementary is rated the worst school in the state. Bart wastes no time in establishing a bad-boy persona, and increases Lisa's popularity by telling people she knows a pop star. With the voice of Ellen Page (Juno).(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Four Great Women and a Manicure
programme.desc: Marge takes Lisa for her first manicure at the salon, where they debate whether a woman can be smart, beautiful and powerful all at the same time. Four tales featuring strong women from history and literature are retold with Selma appearing as Elizabeth I, Lisa as Snow White, Marge as Lady Macbeth and Maggie as Howard Roark from The Fountainhead.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Milhouse moves to Capital City with his mother, forcing Bart to look for new friends - but he finds himself bonding more strongly with Lisa. Meanwhile, Homer resorts to begging to raise money for Marge's anniversary gift.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Who Shot Mr Burns? - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mr Burns angers the residents of Springfield with a plot to block out the sun's light, which would force the town to use the electricity generated by his power plant. The townsfolk plan to fight back, but one mysterious person goes too far and shoots the greedy entrepreneur. Featuring the guest voice of salsa musician Tito Puente.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Who Shot Mr Burns? - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Accusations fly thick and fast in the wake of Mr Burns's shooting, and it is up to Chief Wiggum to discover who pulled the trigger - but plenty of people in Springfield had a motive for wanting the old tyrant dead. Featuring the guest voice of Tito Puente.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Church Street Cobbles in Macclesfield, Cheshire, where foodie Debbie Quinn and housekeeper and cook Isobel Reisen recently opened a B&B above a barber's.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to rock 'n' roll venue the Victoria Bikers Pub in Coalville, Leicestershire, where siblings John and Joy take a more laid back approach to running the business.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Cranberries Hideaway in Kilmington, Devon, where owner Steve and his partner Verity hope the luxury touches will win over their guests.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests head to the Mehfil Hotel in Southall, London, where spiritual host Arun and his sister Rashmi are hoping to bring some Indian hospitality to the competition.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for one last time to find out what they have been paid. Debbie and Isobel are keen to get to the bottom of some of the cleaning comments from Steve and Verity.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Neelesh met 30 years ago on the London Underground, and now with retirement on the horizon they think the time is right to buy a holiday home in their favourite Spanish town of Estepona. The couple have their hearts set on a new build property and have asked Laura Hamilton to help find them the perfect one. Laura lines up various options for their maximum budget of £260,000, but will any impress them enough to make an offer?(n)

programme.title: Spring at Jimmy's Farm
programme.desc: In any other year, as spring arrives on Jimmy Doherty's farm, his 280-acre park would be teeming with families, eager to see his collection of exotic animals. But 2020 is no ordinary year, and while there are no guests, his animals still need caring for. This documentary, filmed by a small crew, offers a unique insight into how Jimmy and his team are managing. In the first episode, lambs, goats and reindeer are all due to give birth, a camel needs to find its mate and Jimmy builds hides and nesting boxes to keep an eye on what the badgers, birds and fish are getting up to.(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including weather.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Compilation of highlights from the past week's programmes, including tips on making light boxes, paper flowers, 3D models, origami and a name sign for a child's room. Plus, memorable moments from Kirstie's other series and ideas for craft projects sent in by viewers.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Archaeologists reveal the mysteries of one of Egypt's greatest female pharaohs, Hatshepsut. The discovery of a sphinx in an ancient quarry sparks an investigation that leads to the temple of Karnak, while analysis of mummified body parts found in a tomb brings up new evidence concerning the lives of women in ancient Egypt.(n)

programme.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets (2017)

programme.title: The Counsellor (2013)

He quickly finds himself out of his depth in a criminal underworld he barely understands, where his actions bring deadly consequences. Ridley Scott's crime thriller, starring Michael Fassbender, Penelope Cruz, Cameron Diaz, Javier Bardem and Brad Pitt.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

The programme also features a one-year-old cat that was hit by a car on its first venture outside the house, leaving it requiring emergency treatment.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with two sets of house-hunters - a couple who were moving into town and another who were set on leaving. programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.desc: Part one of three. Marie gives Debra the silent treatment for being rude to her, so Ray talks the pair into making amends - but as usual in the Barone household, his efforts backfire. Starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of three. Marie and Debra's feud continues, so Ray and Frank use it to their advantage, while Robert tries to get the warring pair to make friends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra accuses Ray of still not being able to read her moods, prompting them to reminisce fondly about the first night they spent together. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy pulls out all the stops as she hosts a traditional Victorian Christmas Eve dinner for the friends. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj endures an anxious day as he worries that a space probe he helped design will not function properly, while Leonard and Sheldon agree to go clothes shopping with Penny and Amy. Comedy, starring Kunal Nayyar, Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon becomes concerned that his comfortable lifestyle is having an impact on his professional progress and is stopping him from achieving scientific greatness, so he asks the gang to make life more difficult for him. Meanwhile, Howard invents an unflattering game about Raj. Starring Jim Parsons and Simon Helberg.(n)

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty bring back fond food memories for guest Russell Howard, whose favourite meal of all time is an unusual Thai-fusion curry he tasted in Sydney on his first comedy tour. No longer on the restaurant's menu, the boys have tracked down the chef who created the dish and got his original recipe. Plus, Jamie creates the ultimate winter warmer, with epic poached chicken and dumplings, mustard sauce and fiery horseradish and Jimmy creates the ultimate takeaway at home - a barbecue kebab house with rotating doner and shish kebabs.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An outbreak of food poisoning at Krusty Burger is traced to barley produced in a nearby town of Norwegian immigrants and results in the mills being shut down, so the out-of-work residents venture to Springfield to find new jobs. However, when xenophobia starts to spread, Mayor Quimby decides to shut the borders.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and Milhouse vie for the coveted role of costumed sidekick Fallout Boy in a new feature film about their comic-book hero Radioactive Man. Featuring the guest voice of Mickey Rooney.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Marge are accused of being bad parents and sent to a special class to resolve the problem, resulting in Bart, Lisa and Maggie being placed in the care of Ned Flanders - who promptly decides to have them baptised.(n)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Simon Cowell provides the voice of the sharp-tongued administrator of a nursery for gifted children who has the job of deciding whether to accept Maggie. As the possibility of successful admission becomes more likely, Lisa feels her position as the intelligent one in the family is being threatened.(n)

programme.title: Nutty Professor II: The Klumps (2000)
programme.desc: Bumbling scientist Sherman Klump is engaged to be married and determined to rid himself of his troublesome alter ego Buddy Love for ever. All his problems look set to be solved by his fiancee's latest DNA experiment - but things are about to spiral out of control. Comedy sequel, starring Eddie Murphy in multiple roles, along with Janet Jackson and Larry Miller.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, revealing how to make the most of kitchen staples and how to be creative with whatever ingredients are available. Here, he cooks up epic cheese-topped burgers with a tangy fresh salsa and shares two colourful classics - super-spinach pancakes and a fabulous one-pan fish.(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather.(n)

programme.title: Night at the Museum: Secret of the Tomb (2014)
programme.desc: Nightwatchman Larry notices that the living exhibits of his museum are behaving strangely. He discovers that the magical artefact that brings them to life is damaged, so he and his friends travel to London's Natural History Museum to find a way to restore it. Fantasy comedy sequel, starring Ben Stiller, Rebel Wilson and Robin Williams in his final film role.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: The actor and writer celebrates everything that is keeping the Great British public entertained at home. Guests include comedians Tim Vine, Mo Gilligan, Harry Hill and Rosie Jones, classical singer Russell Watson, Love Island's Megan Barton Hanson, radio host Roman Kemp, TV presenter Steph McGovern, newsreader Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Made in Chelsea star Jamie Laing, singer Suggs, and more members of the public.(n)

programme.title: VE Day in Colour: Britain's Biggest Party
programme.desc: Using newly recorded accounts and previously unseen archive, this is the story of the day in May 1945 when the fighting finally stopped across Europe and the celebrations began as Britain rejoiced in unity. The stories of ordinary folk are heard, but alongside the happy tales of street parties, fancy dress, and jelly and ice cream are the reflections of those thinking of loved ones who wouldn't return to enjoy a peace that was hard won.(n)

programme.title: Homeland

programme.title: The Other Mother

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club

Every week, the Turner Prize-winning artist hosts the show from his studio, where he creates new pieces, leads masterclasses and finds out how other artists, creatives and celebrities have been spending their time in isolation. He also encourages artists and viewers alike to produce visual representations of this unique time, at the end of which he plans to display the art in an exhibition chronicling the changing moods of Britain in isolation.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Lela's, a California restaurant that is currently being run into the ground by its inexperienced owner. Expectations are high that Freddie and Raj will produce a fantastic menu, but Michael and Caroline are looking for cracks in their perfect relationship. On the final night, Soroya and Jodie are determined to show their guests what they're all about - but things fail to go according to plan.(n)

programme.desc: Norm embarks on a new business venture and his bar-room buddies are keen to get involved - until things begin to go wrong. Comedy, starring George Wendt.(n)

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam tries to make reservations at Boston's most exclusive restaurant in an attempt to impress Diane. Comedy, starring Ted Danson.(n)

programme.desc: A lovestruck Frasier persuades Martin to look round a retirement home because he wants to meet the establishment's attractive manager. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Mel tries to embarrass Niles, and Frasier is aghast to discover the new station owner has copied the decor of his apartment in a bid to appear sophisticated. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Peterborough, 39-year-old land surveyor and part-time musician Muneer hopes to win over his guests with a menu based on Pakistani recipes passed down through his family for generations. The authenticity extends beyond his food, with Muneer seating his guests on the floor of his specially dressed dining room. His guests include driving instructor Christine, hairdresser Ben, fiery Brazilian Rosie and fridge salesman Gregory. While his food receives mixed reviews, His guests discover his master plan for musical success, suffer bad jokes and find out that one of their fellow diners believes in mermaids.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, driving instructor Christine hopes her vegetarian cuisine will grab her the £1,000 prize. But she's worried about being in the firing line, after Rosie compared Muneer's food to vomit on the first night. Now Moony's determined to give Rosie a public dressing down. However, with everyone dreading Christine's meat-free and bland-sounding menu, Rosie may not be the only one who has to bite her lip.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, mobile hairdresser Ben plans a gourmet night. Most of his guests are looking forward to some meat after Christine's vegetarian feast the night before. And after laid-back Muneer and critical Rosie seem to have put their tension behind them at Christine's, Ben's determined to take things up a notch as he entertains his guests with his amazing body art, cooking skills and pet crabs.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fridge salesman Gregory serves up a Spanish menu in Peterborough, accompanied by a charm offensive that he hopes will win him the £1,000 prize. Will it work or will the guests give him the cold shoulder?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fiery Brazilian Rosie is confident that her night will be the best of the week in Peterborough. programme.title: VE Day: The Lost Films
programme.desc: The story of the celebrations on May 8, 1945, marking the end of the Second World War in Europe, told through the eyewitness accounts and cine films of ordinary people. Rarely seen footage captured by amateur film-makers charts the extraordinary events when the entire nation took to the streets, featuring gatherings in central London, village life in Chapeltown on the outskirts of Sheffield and reaction in Tyne and Wear.(n)

programme.desc: Documentary exploring the relationship between the Queen and Prince Charles. The programme charts significant moments in both their lives, from the Queen's coronation when her eldest son was three years old to Charles' divorce. In recent years, Charles has taken on an increasing amount of his mother's royal responsibilities, allowing a glimpse into the kind of king he will eventually become.(n)

programme.title: ABBA: Secrets of their Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Tracing the personal and musical development of the Swedish groupby delving into three of their greatest hits - Mamma Mia, Dancing Queen and The Winner Takes It All. The film discovers exactly what happened in the studio when they were recorded, what inspired them, the cultural and personal circumstances and, crucially, the drama that surrounded them.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Abba Songs

Featuring rare footage of the band, fascinating facts, and the stories of people who have turned their homes into shrines to the four-piece, a woman who named her daughter Abba, and a lady who learned Swedish just to get closer to her heroes.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity 5 Go Motorhoming
programme.desc: Don Warrington, Lesley Joseph, Nick Heyward, Cleo Rocos and Melvyn Hayes forego their usual creature comforts to travel through Wales in motorhomes. Heading towards the peaks of Snowdonia, the gang enjoy the freedom to take in detours and unscheduled stops, including a boat trip in New Quay and a visit to a Welsh vineyard, the village of Portmeirion and a historic mine.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Scotland: A Magical Journey Through the Highlands
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates the stories of epic train journeys through stunning scenery and natural beauty, beginning with a trip from Inverness to Edinburgh. Not long after setting out, the route passes Culloden, the site of the last pitched battle on British soil. It then enters the Cairngorms National Park, home to some of Europe's finest landscapes as well as valleys steeped in Gaelic folklore. The park's station at Aviemore is home to the Strathspey Railway, where Victorian steam locomotives come to life. There is then a climb to the highest point on Britain's railway network before the train winds its way through Fife. Eventually the route crosses the Forth Bridge before arriving in Scottish capital Edinburgh, admired for its architecture and annual arts festival. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue an out-of-control car that hurtles wildly over a major junction and crashes between two trees. When it stops, the driver and passenger, who are miraculously unharmed, turn out to be 12. Police officers in West Yorkshire investigate a rise in cloned cars, a drunken driver has a tall story to tell, and an armed response vehicle pursues a speeding car across Huddersfield.(n)

programme.title: Police: Hour of Duty
programme.desc: Real-time documentary following police officers on duty between 9pm and 10pm. The first episode sees officers responding to a car driving into someone's garden, a woman who claims to have taken an overdose, and a distressed mother who has been attacked by her daughter. Elsewhere, an undercover prepares to bring a prostitution ring to justice, and an investigation crossing county lines requires detectives to contact the crown prosecution service and try and demonstrate they have enough evidence to convict their suspects.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling Derbyshire's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Expendables 2 (2012)
programme.desc: Mercenary leader Barney Ross is recruited by the CIA to recover a top-secret package, only to come to blows with a rival band of hired guns. Barney swears revenge when the other faction kill his protege, and leads his men on a mission to stop their enemies taking over a former Soviet nuclear facility. Action thriller sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone and Jean-Claude Van Damme.(n)

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1959)
programme.desc: An innocent man becomes embroiled in international espionage as he tries to uncover the meaning behind the cryptic last words of a mystery woman, whom he knew for only a brief time before finding her dying in his flat. He must flee for his life from foreign spies and the police, who think he is a murderer. Spy thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Kenneth More, Taina Elg, Barry Jones and James Hayter.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Spying (1964)
programme.desc: A secret formula is stolen by a sinister organisation masterminded by the evil Dr Crow. The British government plans to assign its top agents to deal with the crisis, but owing to staff shortages, has to settle for a bungling spy and his three hapless trainees. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Cribbins, Jim Dale, Eric Barker and Victor Maddern.(n)

programme.title: Akeelah and the Bee (2006)
programme.desc: A clever girl from a poor neighbourhood finds school frustrating and tries to hide her intelligence from her classmates. When she competes in a spelling competition, she is surprised to win and with the aid of a strict coach, sets out to take part in a national tournament and provide her community with something to rally round. Drama, starring Keke Palmer, Laurence Fishburne and Angela Bassett.(n)

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomon's diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichton's novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov.(n)

programme.title: Risen (2016)
programme.desc: A Roman officer and his assistant are charged with investigating what became of Jesus' body following the crucifixion, as the Roman hierarchy fears stories of a resurrected Messiah may lead to an uprising in Jerusalem. However, what they discover during their search forces them to question everything they believe. Biblical drama, starring Joseph Fiennes and Tom Felton.(n)

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles.(n)

programme.title: Bone Tomahawk (2015)
programme.desc: After an outlaw unknowingly leads a band of cannibalistic cave-dwellers into the peaceful town of Bright Hope, the elderly sheriff rounds up a posse to rescue townsfolk abducted by the ferocious tribesmen. Western horror, starring Kurt Russell and Patrick Wilson.(n)

programme.title: 20,000 Days on Earth (2014)
programme.desc: Documentary about musician Nick Cave, exploring his creative process by following him over the course of one day. The film explores his personal archives and features recordings of his sessions with a psychoanalyst, and includes contributions from friends, family and collaborators, including Kylie Minogue and Ray Winstone.(n)

programme.title: The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie (2004)
programme.desc: The plucky sea creature sets out to prove his boss Mr Krabs is innocent of stealing Neptune's crown. However, the missing item has ended up in sinister Shell City, which all sea-dwellers fear and from which few have ever returned. Animated comedy, with the voices of Tom Kenny, Bill Fagerbakke, Clancy Brown and Scarlett Johansson, plus a cameo appearance by David Hasselhoff.(n)

Comedy, with Billy Crystal, Bette Midler and Marisa Tomei.(n)

Period drama from James Ivory, based on EM Forster's novel and starring Helena Bonham Carter, Maggie Smith, Denholm Elliott, Daniel Day-Lewis, Julian Sands and Judi Dench.(n)

What starts as a break at a fun-filled trip to a luxury resort turns into something far more chaotic. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Mark Wahlberg, Mel Gibson and John Lithgow.(n)

programme.title: Chaos (2005)

Crime thriller, starring Jason Statham, Ryan Phillippe and Wesley Snipes.(n)

Starring Chadwick Boseman, Viola Davis, Nelsan Ellis and Octavia Spencer.(n)

programme.desc: A man is haunted by the fact that his late father was a murderer, something no-one will ever let him forget. His problems are further compounded when he accidentally kills a man in self-defence - and realising people will think it was malicious, he decides to hide the body. Drama, starring Dane Clark, Gail Russell and Ethel Barrymore.(n)

Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark.(n)

programme.title: The Ladykillers (1955)

programme.title: The Two Faces of January (2014)

Thriller based on Patricia Highsmith's novel, starring Viggo Mortensen, Kirsten Dunst and Oscar Isaac.(n)

Fact-based drama, starring Taraji P Henson, Octavia Spencer, Janelle Monae and Kevin Costner.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Jamie Laing, Alex Mytton and Sam Thompson get together online to revisit a classic episode and reflect on some of their biggest dating disasters and scandals from years gone by. As rumours of Alex's infidelity reach a distraught Binky, and with more lies yet to come, a dramatic confrontation is inevitable. Meanwhile, Sam thinks he's met the girl of his dreams in Christiana - until he introduces her to Spencer.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity First Dates
programme.desc: TV presenter Esther Rantzen has found life lonely since the death of her husband Desmond Wilcox in 2000, but is worried her formidable reputation can scare men off. She participates in this celebrity version of the show in which singletons meet up with potential partners at a restaurant. Also appearing on this first edition are actor Richard Blackwood (aka EastEnders bad boy Vincent), The Only Way Is Essex reality TV star Jess Wright and 2012 Paralympics table tennis silver medallist Will Bayley.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. They then they whittle the candidates down until only one remains. In the first edition, Aina, a music producer from London, wants to find someone adventurous who she can have a deeper connection with, while Guildford-based designer Mal - who has dated men and women - is curious to see who she will be drawn to when all options are laid bare before her.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Cheshire couple Iman and Heather Ahmed have a new house and want Kirstie Allsopp to help them decorate it with a vintage 1920s-inspired theme. She comes up with ways of helping Iman overcome his dislike of second-hand goods and shows him how to become creative with glass.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Presenters Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help a couple in their quest for a change of scenery. Cardiff pair Graham and Sally Jenkins have been together for 40 years, but they are unable to decide whether they should set up a new home in west Wiltshire, or Martel in the Lot region of south-western France.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Celia Thompson has long dreamed of running an arts and crafts business in the Carcassonne area of France. However, her partner Nick Hares is hesitant about leaving the UK, as his teenage daughters live near Cambridge. He'd rather move to the Waveney Valley, on the border of Suffolk and Norfolk. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton help the couple solve their dilemma.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Karin Lorenz would love to start a new life in south-western France. She wants to find the right house, with scope to run a self-catering business, so she can get it underway before moving there. However, her partner Mike Griffin would prefer to live in Pembrokeshire. Whichever destination they plump for, Karin's mother is set to move with them - as will their two dogs. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin show the pair some tempting properties.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit two sets of house-hunters who had very specific search criteria. Sisters Gemma and Lucy wanted two of everything in their potential new home in Reading, Berkshire, including parking spaces, bathrooms and equal-sized bedrooms, and professional historical architects Colin and Robin had an exacting list of requirements for Phil in Sussex.(n)

programme.title: Phil Spencer's Stately Homes
programme.desc: The property expert visits Belvoir Castle in Leicestershire, searching for a lost railway under the building and trying his hand at modern-day pest control with a medieval twist. He also discovers an aristocratic sex scandal painted on the ceiling and learns the astronomical cost of the original construction work.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salon owner Hannah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Devon, hoping to impress her guests with a menu bursting with taste sensations.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second dinner party in Devon is hosted by funeral director and BMX fanatic Simon, who hopes his big portions and 'man food' will win him the cash prize at the end of the week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Body artist Pascale hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Devon, preparing a Vietnamese menu full of flavour and colour in an effort to impress her guests enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, her cooking attire for the starter leaves little to the imagination, Nick and Simon find some unusual artwork in her bedroom, Hannah discovers an unwanted guest in her main course, and Nick hits a nerve with Ajay.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nick hosts the fourth night in Devon, preparing a menu that he hopes represents his personality - big, bold and brash - although after his argument with Ajay, he is planning on turning down the banter. However, salon owner Hannah is not impressed with Nick's venison main and ladybird-loving Pascale finds the portions a bit too big. But undertaker Simon is happy to polish off any leftovers - is Nick in with a chance of bagging the £1,000 prize?(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent Ajay hopes her nautical-themed evening will see her sail to victory on the fifth and final night in Devon. Along with a gigantic seafood spread, she has also laid on a boat trip around Torquay harbour. However, all does not go quite to plan in the galley. The guests later gather to discover who has won the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mark and Kathy Domaille, owners of Godshill Park Farm on the Isle of Wight, host the week's first round of the B&B competition and invite their guests to take part in clay pigeon shooting. However, dust behind bath taps, a lack of coat-hangers and a breakfast that does not meet expectations take the shine off their stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Teresa and Clive, owners of the Butterfly Guest House in Porthcawl, south Wales, host the next round of the competition, but their status as beginners is confirmed when one couple find hairs in the bathroom. Another pair are shocked to discover there is no view of the sea from their window, but things liven up when the guests go bowling and attend a surprise karaoke party.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Kalpana and Joseph's four-star townhouse in Blackpool, the Address. Cumbrian farmers Ruth and Mike are unhappy their room overlooks a car park and security light, and a trip to the Pleasure Beach results in Teresa calling Mark and Kathy a miserable couple.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competitors head to Cumbria to visit Mike and Ruth's four-star gold-rated Crake Trees Manor. Two of the guests are looking forward to their stay in the plush building, until they discover they have been allocated outdoor accommodation without electricity, water or a toilet.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Mark and Kathy of Godshill Park Farm receive some underpayments, and accusations of game-playing abound before the week's winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Architect Patrick Bradley has come up with an unusual £100,000 house design built out of four 45ft shipping containers, welded together to form a giant cross and cantilevered over the top of a stream on the family farm in Co Derry. His mother is hoping the new home will help her son find a girlfriend, but the small budget and tight schedule soon pose problems. Kevin McCloud follows his progress.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Ashleigh is rushed to St George's by ambulance after twisting her leg while playing football. X-rays reveal that her femur is broken and she will need surgery. Meanwhile, three-year-old Lola visits A&E with her dad after having a seizure, and 91-year-old Betty arrives with severe chest pain. Doctors carry out a blood test in order to find out whether she is having a heart attack and will require life-saving surgery.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Barrie, 78, is rushed in with breathing difficulties and tests reveal he has fluid on his lungs and faces serious medical treatment, while 75-year-old David is treated after falling down a flight of stairs, and 82-year-old Bob is admitted after a DIY accident at home.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson takes on guest captain Johnny Vegas in another round of words-and-numbers chaos. Their team-mates are Alex Brooker and Sara Pascoe, while Claudia Winkleman and Rob Beckett stand in for Rachel Riley on letters-and-numbers duty, and Tom Allen is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. Hosted by Jimmy Carr.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Meanwhile, three-year-old Lola visits A&E with her dad after having a seizure, and 91-year-old Betty arrives with severe chest pain. Doctors carry out a blood test in order to find out whether she is having a heart attack and will require life-saving surgery.(n)

programme.desc: Barrie, 78, is rushed in with breathing difficulties and tests reveal he has fluid on his lungs and faces serious medical treatment, while 75-year-old David is treated after falling down a flight of stairs, and 82-year-old Bob is admitted after a DIY accident at home.(n)
