programme.desc: Dougal takes over as Craggy Island's new milkman, but his predecessor, local womaniser Pat Mustard, returns to exact an explosive revenge on Ted for getting him sacked. Starring Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp learns how to create a flower arrangement for the petite category at the Royal Cornwall Show, under the instruction of master florist Sioned Rowlands.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a BMW 2002 Turbo for a man who has owned it for 30 years, but has been unable to maintain it due to ill health.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to south Wales to rescue a Vauxhall Astra GTE for ex-boy racer Huw. Tim turns to 21st-century science to create the GTE's irreplaceable hubcaps, while Fuzz sticks to what he knows best by rebuilding the high-performance engine. With the car finally ready, the result is revealed to a shocked Huw via a crafty and complicated set-up.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Eastbourne on the south coast to restore a 1962 Mini Cooper. This particular model is the property of farmer Keith, who has raced and rallied cars for most of his life, until being struck down with serious heart problems. Although his racing days are now over, Keith cannot let go of his beloved Mini, even though it has been rotting away in a shed for years.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.sub-title: Lake District and Scottish Highlands Revisits
programme.desc: House-hunting show with Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer. The property experts catch up with two couples who hoped to realise dreams of finding a new home and business. Catherine and Mark wanted to move from the Midlands to the Lake District with plans to set up a hot-tub company, while Christine and Keith gave up life in Cambridgeshire to go in search of a hotel to run in the Highlands.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Maria, who had moved back to the UK after 10 years in Switzerland and wanted a home near the Thames. Rosie and Andy were looking to swap their busy city lives for a calmer existence by the sea in Devon. Were the property experts able to navigate the choppy waters of house-hunting and find them the perfect home?(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another quartet of B&B owners battle it out to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money, beginning with Paul Billington and Flora Loizou from Silverwood Lodge in Cambridgeshire. The couple get a frosty response when one guest learns her hosts do not live on-site, while another is left in hysterics by an unexpected discovery.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group sets off for Great Yarmouth to stay in Sue Dodd's dog-friendly Seashells Guesthouse. Spirits run high with a trip to the beach and a game of bingo, but Nancy's complaints over dog hairs on the sheets and smoking in the rooms bring down the light-hearted mood during dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: American ex-banker Nancy Hampson hosts at the Old Court Guesthouse in Bath, but her vocal criticisms of the week's first two establishments have made her no friends among her fellow competitors. Paul Billington is annoyed that the bed he and wife Flora Loizou are given is not big enough, but is more impressed when taken for food at a quirky local restaurant.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests stay with Caroline Scott, the owner of the Dean B&B near Longniddry, East Lothian The picturesque house and breathtaking views across the Firth of Forth win everyone over, but underwear in the drawers, cobwebs on the curtains and a messy breakfast service seriously hamper the host's chances of success.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Sue from Seashells in Great Yarmouth confronts Nancy Hampson about the controversial topic of smoking in bedrooms, while Paul Billington and Flora Loizou are held to account about their low mark for the Old Court Guesthouse.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Performing arts teacher Helen hosts the first dinner party in Sheffield, but her flamboyant cooking does not go to according to plan.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Design and technology teacher Claire hosts the second day of the competition in Sheffield, and hopes her Asian-inspired menu with a spot of clay model-making will impress her fellow diners enough to net her the points she'll need to secure the cash prize. However, former chef Simon Hollings proves difficult to faze, and the eccentric Helen irritates the other contestants with another audacious display at the dinner table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Service administrator Monika hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Sheffield. She treats her guests to a traditional Bulgarian night inspired by her homeland, in the hope of impressing them enough to win the £1,000 prize money. However, some of the guests are unsure about her Bulgarian delicacies, and the host must fight for control when the two teachers go head-to-head and when Simon makes sexist jokes.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Day four in Sheffield sees musician Ivor's turn to host, and he is determined to make it a fun evening with a menu inspired by 1980s pop and a performance by his new romantic band.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Former chef Simon hosts the final meal in Sheffield, but is unlikely to get an easy ride from his guests following all the criticism he has dished out at the earlier soirees. He also has to contend with the feud that's brewing between Claire and Helen, and when things boil over between the women, Monika doesn't know where to hide.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tracy and Steve Fox want to build an 'urban shed' featuring two studios, three bedrooms and a double-height space with a walkway, made out of industrial-style materials including corrugated cement fibreboard and 20ft panels of polycarbonate sheeting. Having discovered an old milk yard tucked away behind some flats and shops in south-east London, their long-held desire to build a highly unconventional family home and workspace begins. But their choice of unorthodox materials proves challenging and costly. Kevin McCloud follows their progress.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton rush through the traffic when they are called to a hotel where a woman is about to give birth.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: More than 60,000 people in Wiltshire have mental health issues, but with support services across the UK being cut it is falling to the emergency services to pick up the pieces. This edition focuses on Wiltshire Police and South West Ambulance call handlers who regularly find themselves counselling people with severe mental health episodes, including one who calls 999 every three seconds claiming he is the archangel Michael.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors carry out tests on 77-year-old Martin, who was rushed to St George's after collapsing at home and suffered two further seizures in A&E. As he waits to find out what is causing him problems, his wife Margaret talks about their life together. Meanwhile, 83-year-old widower Harry arrives with shortness of breath, chest pains and dizziness after collapsing while shopping. Paediatric nurse Kelly, who loves working with children, treats six-year-old Lilly, who has cut her head after falling off her scooter.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: West Midlands paramedic Rob Armstrong and technician Mike Horton rush through the traffic when they are called to a hotel where a woman is about to give birth.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Mojito, a Cuban cafe in Brooklyn, New York. The restaurant was the shared dream of two newlyweds who have separated since its opening, and whose constant public quarrelling is distracting staff and driving customers away.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors carry out tests on 77-year-old Martin, who was rushed to St George's after collapsing at home and suffered two further seizures in A&E. As he waits to find out what is causing him problems, his wife Margaret talks about their life together. Paediatric nurse Kelly, who loves working with children, treats six-year-old Lilly, who has cut her head after falling off her scooter.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Soon-to-be parents Iman and Heather Ahmed want a vintage-inspired nursery for their baby, but Heather has never used a sewing machine. Can Kirstie help them create the bedroom that they dream of for their first child?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Graham Hartridge and Maggie Rogers have been together for 18 months and fancy an adventure. He would love to buy a place in the south-west of France, but she is nervous of taking the plunge and would prefer Devon - much closer to family and friends. Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help with their property search.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are on the Wirral, where Simon has a plan to turn a chunky chain into a standard lamp and Henry picks up a 1963 Ford Anglia to get it back to its best.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team is near Preston, where Simon O'Brien restores a spotlight he believes may be from a German U-boat while Henry Cole gets creative with an aircraft jump seat.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. As if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: In Hertfordshire, Charlie Luxton helps Roz and Mick Abrahams build a compact contemporary home at the bottom of their garden. Mick and Roz are downsizing from a huge house, and designing a home half the size causes numerous headaches. Charlie shares some bright ideas to demonstrate that, even with a smaller home, it is still possible to think big.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Miners Arms in Nenthead, Cumbria, where sisters Alison Baker and Lynda Clark hope to impress guests with their eco credentials.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and James Widdas run the Rose and Crown in Edgworth, near Bolton, and hope to provide boutique on a budget, but veterans Alison and Lynda are quick to find fault.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nigel and Pok U Regan hope to impress their fellow hoteliers with their east-meets-west atmosphere at Bingham Townhouse, a B&B and Cantonese restaurant in Nottinghamshire.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Float by Boat near Rugby, partners Kev Argent and Tor Johnson run mindfulness breaks from their narrowboat, but Alison and Lynda are not keen on the tiny shared bathroom.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day in the competition sees Andrew and James Widdas from the Rose and Crown confront their critics about their polyester bedding. Nigel and Pok U from the Bingham Townhouse have some explaining to do when it comes to their breakfast service, while Kev and Tor from Float by Boat are left in tears by the payments they receive.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien attempt to restore items including an old cheese press, and a 1980s motorbike almost gets the better of Henry and his restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Copper Beech House in Malvern, Worcestershire, a B&B run by ex-professional rugby union player Steve Lloyd and his partner Kathy Leather.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offer recipes, tips and hacks tailored for the Covid-19 outbreak, revealing how to make the most of kitchen staples and how to be creative with whatever ingredients are available. From ingenious ideas on what to do with forgotten frozen food, to recipes drawn entirely from the store cupboard, Jamie shows how to make frugal nutritious and delicious food.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Natasha Cargill wants to build a home shaped like two enormous periscopes in rural Norfolk, but to obtain planning permission, she has to ensure not only that the materials are sustainable, but also agree to measure the transportation used to deliver them. If these strict criteria are not met, she won't be allowed to live there, and to add to the pressure, she has just £330,000 to spend on construction and six months to complete the project. Kevin McCloud follows her progress.(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The Morgan Motor Company opens its doors to reveal how its £90,000 Plus Six sports car is made, with 170 craftspeople using classic coach-building techniques.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harriet Kemsley take on special guest team captain Alan Carr and Catherine Tate in the classic words-and-numbers quiz, while Adam Buxton joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Thirty-seven-year-old Robert arrives at St George's Hospital, London after colliding with a car on his motorbike. Scans show he has a bleed on the brain, a broken jaw, and two missing teeth, and he faces potentially life-changing injuries and extensive surgery. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mary collapsed in the street on the way to work and hit her head. Doctors need to establish whether it is simple fainting, or whether something more sinister is to blame, with tests revealing she will need a pacemaker fitted. Plus, 27-year-old Jessica comes to A&E with a wound that will not heal, after having a lumpectomy to remove breast cancer tissue two months ago.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Beat the Internet with John Robins
programme.desc: The comedian hosts as members of the public are pitted against each other in a quest to predict how search engines respond to a series of questions.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food takes a gastronomic tour along the canal systems of southern France, from Bordeaux to Marseille. He samples seafood and regional cuisine, as well as exploring a variety of traditional recipes, including artichoke salad with poached egg, eels in cider, and bass with beurre blanc.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food explores the banks of the River Garonne on board a 100-year-old converted barge, and savours the delights of French provincial cooking including magret de canard and petit sale. Other dishes featured are clams in white garlic sauce, eels in parsley and garlic butter, and steak tartare.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Louisiana, Danielle calls the shots from HQ when the guys find themselves in over their heads, and Frank strikes a side deal that leaves a permanent mark.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank finally get access to their white whale pick, while in Texas, the guys meet a collector who has filled an entire town with his mammoth stockpile.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees bidders in Lewes battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: In Houston, Eddie faces the 'grim reaper' in a crawfish challenge featuring the fearsome reaper pepper. He also devours some southern comfort food and pulls a plane down a runway.(n)

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie Hall follows in Rocky's footsteps with physical challenges at a famed Philadelphia boxing gym - and his very own hunger games in the infamous Philly Cheesesteak Challenge.(n)

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: People forced out of their homes in Honduras by the notorious MS-13 gang plead their case for asylum in America. Officers discover 3,000lb of marijuana hidden in a cargo truck.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Russia as the motoring magazine returns, taking part in a race across the urban landscape of St Petersburg. James defends the honour of the car in a very small Renault, Richard tries to prove that pedalling is faster by riding a bicycle, while Jeremy attempts to demonstrate that water is the quickest way across town by piloting a hovercraft. To make matters more complicated, all three presenters are racing not only one another but also the Stig, who is making his way toward the finish line by public transport. Plus, the new Lamborghini Huracan is put through its paces and singer-songwriter Ed Sheeran takes to the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters embark on a road trip across Australia's Northern Territory in fast, luxurious GT cars, with Jeremy Clarkson choosing a BMW M6 Gran Coupe, Richard Hammond picking a Bentley Continental GT V8 and James May opting for a Nissan GT-R. The trio set out on an adventure that takes in crocodile-infested rivers, a race around a vast opencast mine and an encounter with one of the world's longest lorries, concluding with an unusual challenge involving a farm, their cars and 4,000 cows. Plus, 24 star Kiefer Sutherland takes to the track in the Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the comedy panel show, which sees chef and MasterChef judge John Torode, comedian Russell Howard and actress Katherine Parkinson compete to have their pet hates banished for ever. Their gripes include massive pepper grinders, grumpy kids and DJs who join in at the end of a song.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites comedy actress Catherine Tate, X Factor contender-turned-television host Rylan Clark-Neal and veteran actor Nigel Havers to vent their spleen about people and objects that upset them. Their gripes include hipster restaurants, set-up paparazzi shoots and Labour Party leader Jeremy Corbyn.(n)

programme.title: Room 101

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver admires ginger seals at Walton-on-the-Naze, while Nick Crane examines the area's fossil record. Miranda Krestovnikoff explores research into the thornback ray on the Thames Estuary and how it aids conservation. The team also visits Felixstowe, the UK's busiest container port, where HM Revenue and Customs uncover a stash of cocaine smuggled among furniture.(n)

programme.title: Deadliest Crash: Disaster at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary examining what caused Pierre Levegh's Mercedes to crash into the crowd during the 1955 Le Mans 24-hour race, killing more than 80 people - an accident that remains the worst in the history of motor racing. Among the theories discussed are the use of untested technologies by the car's manufacturers, the addition of a lethal fuel additive and allegations of reckless driving by future Formula One world champion Mike Hawthorn, who won the race with Jaguar team partner Ivor Bueb. Narrated by Jack Deam.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories surrounding the death of Hermann Goering, who managed to cheat the hangman by taking a cyanide capsule on the day before his scheduled death.(n)

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Heavy haulage experts Ian Bagladi and Jamie Turnbull need to move a classical but abandoned 63-year-old, 70-tonne locomotive, through the English countryside.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a car auction business. A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Following the wildlife that inhabits the famous American national park, a lost world of deep forests, vast plains and bubbling geysers. The first episode focuses on a variety of species as they struggle to survive the rigours of a freezing six-month winter, revealing that although the herds of elk and bison are weakened by the cold, one animal gets stronger - the wolf.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Summer returns, transforming the landscape of the famous national park from a frozen wilderness into the ideal environment for newborn animals. As temperatures rise, grizzly bears teach their young the secrets of survival - but even at this time of year, snow can sweep down from the mountains without warning.(n)

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: An early dusting of snow signals the end of autumn, a time when the forests are a blaze of red and gold and the animals prepare for the coming cold. The elk face hungry wolves as they move down from the mountains to feed in the valleys, while beavers rush to defend their habitat against the destructive ice, repairing dams and stocking underwater larders before the waters freeze over.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Folk performances by artists including Pete Seeger, Tim Buckley, Joan Baez, Bob Dylan and Leonard Cohen.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Music from the flower power era, featuring bands including Herman's Hermits, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, the Alan Price Set, Manfred Mann and the Rolling Stones.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profile of the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a year in the existence of 1,000-year-old Windsor Castle and its inhabitants, from the housekeeper and chefs to the Queen herself, who has allowed her public and private life to be documented more extensively than ever before. The state visit of former President of France Jacques Chirac sees the staff organise a banquet and a performance of a West End musical inside the building - but protesters outside threaten to mar proceedings.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores the disused tube station Down Street in Mayfair, that played a crucial tactical role during the Second World War, and tours Rotterdam's new Centraal station with the man who designed it.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Constructing the Gotthard Base Tunnel in Switzerland, the longest rail tunnel in the world, running for 57 kilometres through the Alps.(n)

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre uses the SAS archives to examine the history of the British Army special forces unit combining documents, unseen footage and interviews with its founding members to tell the remarkable story behind an extraordinary fighting force. In the first edition, he looks at its formation in the heat of the north African desert in 1941, when eccentric young British army officer David Archibald Stirling had a vision for a new kind of war in which the enemy was attacked from behind their own lines.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: British bombers are unable to hit their targets precisely and attack German cities under cover of darkness, while the Blitz in Britain brings public demand for revenge strikes, and Arthur Harris takes charge of Bomber Command.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Churchill's plan to attack Germany through Italy. Although considered the simplest military solution, the Allies were to face formidable opposition, while the Americans met the Wehrmacht for the first time. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffe's bombing raids on Britain's provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The underground base with the power to destroy a nation, the site linked with an attempt to kill a vicious dictator, and an ancient world discovered in the open American wilderness.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A secretive underground base that concealed the power to destroy nations, and a sprawling industrial relic clinging to the edge of an unforgiving waterfall.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A marooned ironclad structure off the coast of England that hides a dramatic story of war and resilience, and a facility in the heart of America that helped tear apart a nation.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Forgotten relics from the defence of America's homeland, concealed by the banks of the Mississippi, and a concrete behemoth on the French coast that proved invulnerable to attacks.(n)

programme.title: Saluting Dad's Army

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: British troops save Edith and Rene from execution, and the pair are released into the woods. Meanwhile, the Germans decide to head for Spain, and Michelle joins Crabtree in a duet. Classic wartime sitcom, starring Gorden Kaye and Carmen Silvera.(n)

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp.(n)

programme.title: History's Ultimate Spies
programme.desc: After the death of Cardinal Wolsey in 1530, Thomas Cromwell became Henry VIII's 'lap dog'. In order to do the king's bidding, he established a network of spies that steered Henry through his troubles with Rome, divorces, assassinations attempts and the establishment of a new religion. as this documentary reveals, though cunning and ambitious, none of these achievements proved sufficient to preserve his life.(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston talks to author Joy Hancox about the alchemical roots of London, the design of famous buildings such as The Globe theatre and the mysterious figure of English poet John Byrom. He also meets a modern day potion-maker.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: A look at the early stages of genocide at the camp, examining the role of commander Rudolf Hoess in the process. Despite later claiming he was only following orders, Hoess was instrumental in the development of Auschwitz for mass murder, arranging for Soviet prisoners to be brought in as construction workers and scouring Europe for more victims to kill. Featuring contributions from survivors.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germany's initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitler's life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Series exploring ruined engineering projects that have been put to alternative use, including the site of the Chernobyl disaster and a Nazi holiday complex.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The world's most impressive disused railway bridges, including the biggest wooden trestle bridge in the world, which was closed down due to hurricane damage.(n)

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: The historical importance of freight trains, including four-mile long vehicles transporting enough iron ore to build two Eiffel towers.(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Monsoon
programme.desc: Colin Salmon narrates a documentary exploring how the lives of humans and animals across a vast area of the planet are affected by the transforming power of the monsoon rains. The end of the dry season in northern Australia sees 300,000 flying foxes gathering to drink while running a gauntlet of freshwater crocodiles, while in Sumatra a female orang-utan guides her youngster to where she knows a fig tree fruits at this time of year. At the Delhi stock exchange, the price of rice escalates every day the rains fail to materialise and priests submerge themselves in water and beseech Varuna, god of water, to come.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: A look at how the 20,000 islands in the region became colonised by humans and animals. For the Polynesians, the Pacific's first people, their journeys constituted a feat of navigational ingenuity, while animals often had to rely on violent forces of nature, such as cyclones and tsunamis, to reach the shores.(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard.(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: Examining the complex system the Nazis set up to implement the extermination of the Jewish race throughout Europe from 1942, with Auschwitz as the hub. The programme reveals that at other camps, including Treblinka, Belzec and Sobibor, rival commanders participated in the genocide with enthusiasm and shared ideas for the best method of mass murder.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring ship graveyards, including Scapa Flow in Orkney, where German warships were scuttled after the First World War, and Mallows Bay, home to 200 wrecks colonised by wildlife.(n)

A treasure hunt brings out Glen Wilkie's feelings about Serine Daou, Sonya Smith dislikes some of the gluten-free food and, for the third night in a row, Sabi Phagura is served a plate of meat she will not eat.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth dinner party in Reading sees fitness fanatic Sabi Phagura putting her healthy regimen to one side to host a bhangra-themed evening. Unfortunately, neither the traditional Indian food on the table nor the entertainment seems to impress the guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Corset-maker Serine Daou hosts the final night of the culinary competition in Reading, Berkshire, and hopes to impress with her burlesque-themed party. When the gourmet burgers she serves fail to impress, she must rely on the evening's entertainment to put her back on course for the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Leeds compete to host the best dinner party, beginning with construction manager Martin and his wife Amanda, who are determined to win. They are rivalled by competitive engaged couple Amy and Nico who impress with their entertainment and Hannah and Nick, who try to prove their doubters wrong by cooking duck to order.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackpool compete for the £1,000 prize, beginning with practising Buddhist Antonio and former redcoat Jason, who serve up a sushi starter. A discovery on day two at the home of fireman Daz and his wife Kim raises a few eyebrows, while the final night sees foodies Martin and Kate prepare an authentic Thai menu and a chocolate dessert.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Roni and Malcolm from Exeter search for a property in Granada in southern Spain, as they look for a rural retreat on a budget of £150,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her property expertise to help find Neil and Linda from Lanarkshire a rustic holiday home around the picturesque town of Antequera in southern Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lecturer Maxine and paramedic team leader Cormac got married in the scenic northern region of the Dordogne in France, and now want Ben Hillman's help to find them their dream holiday home there. They have a budget of just over £100,000 and would ideally like a property with lots of outside space, just a short walk from the town of Riberac.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Jonnie Irwin help Cambridgeshire couple Wendy and Pete, who cannot decide whether they want to move to Great Yarmouth in Norfolk or Almeria in Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Iain from south Wales are looking to buy a holiday home in Ibiza after they fell in love with the island on a family holiday six years ago. They're especially drawn to the pretty town of Santa Eularia and have set their hearts on somewhere with a sea view, so call upon the skills of property expert Ben Hillman to find them the home of their dreams for their £275,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to reverse the fortunes of the Mixing Bowl diner in Bellmore, New York, where a talented cook's work is being undone by the manager's marketing plans - putting the finances in dire straits.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef heads to the Seascape restaurant in the village of Islip on Long Island, New York, in a bid to change the fortunes of its owner, who is nearly $1million in debt. Gordon is dismayed to discover unprotected meat and fish left next to mouldy pesto jars in the kitchen and plans to close down the eatery until radical changes are made.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on Race Across the World, Killing Eve, Grayson's Art Club, CBeebies Bedtime Stories, This Morning and BBC News on Trump and disinfectant.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: The comedian fights the good fight on behalf of the British public, tackling viewers' consumer problems, no matter how big or small. In this edition, Made in Chelsea's Jamie Laing helps Joe launch the UK's first ever consumer rights rave, while the show's fearless assistant Mark Silcox tests the slogan of a high street bank. Joe also infiltrates hackable smart tech and launches a musical raid on the offices of the world's largest online retailer. Plus, comedian Lucy Beaumont spies on CCTV itself.(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: A Cornish woman with a love of dancing dates a man who runs a pasty business in Plymouth, a guest in her seventies is hoping to meet someone like Sean Connery, and an estate agent claims to be looking for a workman with a lot of pockets.(n)

programme.title: VE Day in Colour: Britain's Biggest Party
programme.desc: Using newly recorded accounts and previously unseen archive, this is the story of the day in May 1945 when the fighting finally stopped across Europe and the celebrations began as Britain rejoiced in unity. The stories of ordinary folk are heard, but alongside the happy tales of street parties, fancy dress, and jelly and ice cream are the reflections of those thinking of loved ones who wouldn't return to enjoy a peace that was hard won.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Egypt's Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Archaeologists reveal the mysteries of one of Egypt's greatest female pharaohs, Hatshepsut. The discovery of a sphinx in an ancient quarry sparks an investigation that leads to the temple of Karnak, while analysis of mummified body parts found in a tomb brings up new evidence concerning the lives of women in ancient Egypt.(n)

programme.title: What's It Like to Catch Coronavirus?
programme.desc: The lockdown continues and people practise social distancing in an effort to avoid Covid-19. However, for those who have caught the virus - what is it actually like? With some estimates suggesting up to 80 per cent of the population may be affected, it's vital to understand how the symptoms, from mild to life-threatening, affect individuals. Here, people from all over Britain document their experiences of living with the disease. The personal stories reveal how different people respond in different ways, and how the virus impacts on people's mental and economic health.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie: Keep Crafting and Carry On
programme.desc: Compilation of highlights from the past week's programmes, including tips on making light boxes, paper flowers, 3D models, origami and a name sign for a child's room. Plus, memorable moments from Kirstie's other series and ideas for craft projects sent in by viewers.(n)

programme.title: Reasons to Be Cheerful with Matt Lucas
programme.desc: The actor and writer celebrates everything that is keeping the Great British public entertained at home. Guests include comedians Tim Vine, Mo Gilligan, Harry Hill and Rosie Jones, classical singer Russell Watson, Love Island's Megan Barton Hanson, radio host Roman Kemp, TV presenter Steph McGovern, newsreader Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Made in Chelsea star Jamie Laing, singer Suggs, and more members of the public.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Jonathan and Michael prepare for a wedding day with 45 guests. With the first guests of the season about to turn up, the pool continues to be a problematic nightmare for Mandy and Steve. Dick's wife Angel shows some clever, cheap, wedding tips - but will Dick and the kids like them?(n)

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp's weekly project is to design and make a name sign for her son's wall, and she shows how to make a cushion out of a jumper and tries her hand at Mexican tin art.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Church Street Cobbles in Macclesfield, Cheshire, where foodie Debbie Quinn and housekeeper and cook Isobel Reisen recently opened a B&B above a barber's.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Peterborough, 39-year-old land surveyor and part-time musician Muneer hopes to win over his guests with a menu based on Pakistani recipes passed down through his family for generations. The authenticity extends beyond his food, with Muneer seating his guests on the floor of his specially dressed dining room. His guests include driving instructor Christine, hairdresser Ben, fiery Brazilian Rosie and fridge salesman Gregory. While his food receives mixed reviews, His guests discover his master plan for musical success, suffer bad jokes and find out that one of their fellow diners believes in mermaids.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra grudgingly agrees to let Ray have more involvement in the daily decision-making process, giving him the freedom to shop for grocery items he likes and choose where the family will go on holiday. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton.(n)

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra, Ray and Amy get trapped at Frank and Marie's house during a snowstorm, but they actually find themselves having a good time - until an unguarded comment alters the mood. Comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Monica Horan.(n)

programme.sub-title: And the Dish Ran Away with the Spoon
programme.desc: Double-length episode. Donny issues a lawsuit against Daphne for jilting him at the altar, and Mel plots revenge against Niles after he abandoned his marriage to her. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce, Jane Leeves and Saul Rubinek.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Alan and Sandra from Carlisle search for a retirement retreat in Alicante. However, traffic enthusiast Alan has an unusual request - he wants the new home to be near a busy road.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp revisit house-hunters in Norfolk, first featured in January 2014. Young-at-heart pensioners Jon and Peggy were relocating from Derby to fulfil their dream of living by the sea - and with £450,000 to spend, they had an exacting list of criteria that had to be met. Meanwhile, Yvonne and Lisa from Leeds had reached the end of their tether after spending more than a year searching for a home in a rural district. A year on, how are both pairs getting on?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: It is the third phase of the selection process and the celebrity recruits are put in specially selected pairs to test their ability to work as a unit. Each partnership must eat, sleep and operate as a duo as they are put through brutal tasks, including a paired abseil down a 60-metre cliff. The course front runners - reality TV Star Joey Essex and Paralympian Lauren Steadman - discover that although they may shine as individuals, it's unity that matters, and their lack of teamwork means they're both at risk of failure.(n)

programme.sub-title: Table for Too Many - Part One

programme.sub-title: An Old Flame with a New Wick
programme.desc: Charlie agrees to meet an old flame, but is stunned to learn his former girlfriend, Jill, is now a man. As a relationship blossoms, the rest of the family waits to see how she will handle the inevitable revelation.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: I Remember the Coat Room, I Just Don't Remember You
programme.desc: Judith's sister Liz (guest star Teri Hatcher) turns up at Jake's birthday party, to the delight of Charlie, who remembers her from a previous encounter. Humiliatingly, it seems she does not recall him - and worse, is determined to seduce Alan. Meanwhile, Rose goes to a wedding in a clown costume.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: Hey, I Can Pee Outside in the Dark
programme.desc: Judith suggests that Jake see a therapist to help him get over his parents' divorce. Charlie and Alan are unconvinced the split is to blame for the boy's mood, while Evelyn uses the situation as an excuse to offload her own resentment. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Angus T Jones.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie forms a relationship with Alan's divorce attorney, whose demands quickly become excessive even for the womanising musician - but the short-lived flirtation threatens to jeopardise his brother's chances of obtaining the settlement he wants. Comedy, guest starring Heather Locklear.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: My Doctor Has a Cow Puppet
programme.desc: Alan starts sleepwalking, and Charlie feels duty-bound to cure him. Meanwhile, Jake starts seeing a therapist. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake's inappropriate remarks at a meeting of Judith's divorcee support group prompt his furious mother to tell Alan she does not want her son living with Charlie any longer. The womanising musician goes to the next meeting and struggles to convince the ladies he has a good influence on his nephew.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie makes a doctor's appointment for a vasectomy - but decides to freeze some sperm in case he ever wants children. However, the simple procedure of producing a sample seems destined to fail as his family and friends manage to thwart one attempt after another. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie hurts Alan's feelings by asking him to stay out of the house while he has friends over, so Rose steps in to cheer him up - but finds his gloom hard to dispel. Sean Penn, Elvis Costello and Harry Dean Stanton guest star.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is encouraged by Charlie to return a keepsake he stole from Judith during their divorce. In doing so, he learns she has a new boyfriend - a revelation that makes him even more depressed. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen and Jon Cryer.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake faces suspension from school, so Charlie tries to help by romancing his teacher Miss Pasternak - but isn't prepared for the repercussions of his decision to end the relationship. Starring Charlie Sheen and Angus T Jones.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.sub-title: Can't Pay? My Nightmare Ex Wife
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents face a raft of excuses and stalling tactics as they try to recover £3,000 from a young debtor in Manchester, and a Kent businessman tries to protect his company from his ex-wife by claiming that it belongs to his sister. A Staffordshire optician who owes money to a supplier claims that the equipment he was sold was faulty, while enforcement agents in north London are forced to mediate between a tenant who claims she is being evicted for having children and a landlord who says she wanted to leave in the hope of getting a council house.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on stories featured in previous episodes, including Kent couple Rachel and Chris, who were pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant broke down and she stopped paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. Was he able to sell his property at least, or was he tempted to become a landlord once again?(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting alarming tales from all sides of the buy-to-let system, drawing attention to stories of unruly tenants who refuse to pay their dues and rogue landlords who have failed to live up to their responsibilities. programme.desc: Following a tip-off, Harrow Enforcement officers are shocked to discover an industrial-scale cannabis farm in a house. The house is in terrible shape, but the young tenants promise they will clear it all up and keep the noise down. programme.desc: The detectives are concerned to hear a convict charged with the rape and murder of a prostitute has been paroled, and decide to keep tabs on him. programme.desc: A woman is accused of abusing her teenage stepson - who is later implicated in a murder. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Ice-T.(n)

programme.desc: An architect kills his business partner, and comes up with an elaborate scheme to make Columbo first suspect him, then reject him as the murderer. The villain begins by teasing the detective with hints on where he has hidden the body - hoping that when Columbo fails to find a corpse, he will look elsewhere for a killer. Crime drama, directed by and starring Peter Falk. With Patrick O'Neal and Janis Paige.(n)

programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.desc: A woman who has devoted her life to running her family's museum is outraged when her brother decides to sell it. Knowing of someone with criminal connections, she arranges to have her brother killed. However, just when she starts to think she has got away with it, a certain shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Joyce Van Patten, Celeste Holm and Tim O'Connor.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns.(n)

Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Eddie Albert, Suzanne Pleshette and John Kerr.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Detective Eames is forced to re-examine the circumstances behind her officer husband's murder when his former partner is killed on the job, and revisits a life that she thought long buried. Crime drama, starring Kathryn Erbe and Vincent D'Onofrio.(n)

programme.desc: An elderly woman suffering from Alzheimer's disease breaks into an apartment. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is found raped and murdered in Central Park - and as her work involved contacting sexual partners of HIV patients, the detectives soon have a long list of suspects. The discovery of a second body raises fears that they are dealing with a serial killer - and the trail leads to a man who has no knowledge of the crimes, but is distantly connected to the first victim and her husband.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler and Dr Huang try to prove a convicted serial killer is responsible for the rape and murder of another woman, whose assailant was never identified. In doing so, they hope to give the victim's parents a sense of closure - but face a race against time as the man is due to be executed in three days. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni, BD Wong and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Stabler's wife tells him about a teenage rape victim at the local hospital who is too frightened to go to the police. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a woman killed in a fall from her art studio rooftop. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Two thieves discover the skeletal remains of a woman who was believed to have died in the September 11 attacks on the World Trade Centre. It transpires that a valuable and unusual ring has been stolen from the body, and the team suspects foul play. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Elisabeth Rohm and Sam Waterston.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are assigned to investigate the execution-style killing of a prominent businessman, and discover his wife was having an affair. However, further evidence emerges, suggesting a different motive - and an unlikely conspirator. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team try to find out who is responsible for sending a parcel containing a pair of blue human eyeballs to the home of a petty officer who works in Intelligence. However, the recipient of the organs commits suicide during the course of the inquiry, which leads the investigators to suspect one of his lecturers, who is working for the CIA in Panama.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is killed while competing in a swimsuit contest, raising fears she may have fallen victim to a jealous rival, or even a stalker. Gibbs' investigation leads to the discovery that the victim was taking yoga classes - and this provides him with the breakthrough he needs to solve the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the fatal shooting of two professors, which occurred soon after one of the academics campaigned against the treatment of women in the Middle East. Their inquiries lead them to a Muslim with strong beliefs - and an extreme dislike of females. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A police officer is shot dead and the chief suspect's confession appears to bring an end to the matter, but his media-savvy lawyer presents a case accusing the force of a string of errors - as well as perceived racism. Guest starring Gregory Hines, in his final acting role before his death.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the fatal stabbing of a taxi driver whose body was left under his vehicle. The finger of suspicion points to a man who took a drunken ride in the cab on the same night - an ex-convict who is also a gifted writer - and his protege, whose fingerprints were found on a book and cigar left at the scene. Saul Rubinek, JK Simmons and Sonia Braga guest star alongside Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is murdered, but it is not long before the prime suspect is also found dead. Tim and Abby discover a clue to both killings in an e-mail that suggests the victims were involved in a game with deadly consequences. Drama, starring Sean Murray and Pauley Perrette.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine suspected of murdering a girl is killed in Iraq, and a search of his personal belongings reveals her body. Most members of the team believe they have found conclusive evidence of his guilt - but Gibbs suspects there is more to the situation than meets the eye.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when the body of a US Navy lieutenant, who disappeared over a year earlier, is found buried in a cemetery by grounds-keepers relocating caskets. Gibbs and McGee must pass a psych evaluation to officially resume working.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a five-year-old girl is found, leading to the revelation that a homicidal paedophile is on the loose. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The rape of a woman in a coma leads the team to question a doctor whose stem cell research is funded by a billionaire with Parkinson's disease. They discover the tycoon is working in tandem with the medic to find a cure, but the pair's unethical procedures eventually come under scrutiny in court. Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.sub-title: DIY SOS: Homes for Veterans - Part One
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team take on their most ambitious project to date and transform a whole street of run-down empty properties in Manchester into homes for veterans. They are joined by the local community, as well as Prince William and Prince Harry, who volunteer their assistance on site. In the first edition of the two-part special, the team builds houses for two former soldiers who fought in Iraq and Afghanistan and are suffering from post traumatic stress disorder. However, with the number of volunteers lacking and the buildings proving to be trickier than expected, the build is becoming the toughest yet.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Raisin
programme.sub-title: Agatha Raisin and the Quiche of Death
programme.desc: Ashley Jensen plays the title role in this quirky crime drama, based on the novels of MC Beaton. Bored with her new life in the Cotswolds, former London-based PR whizz Agatha Raisin re-doubles efforts to ingratiate herself with the local community. However, when entering its annual quiche-making competition, she inadvertently finds herself a suspect in a murder case. With her reputation tarnished and hopes of romance with a dashing ex-army neighbour seemingly thwarted, Agatha feels she has little choice but to clear her name by unmasking the real culprit. With Hermione Norris, Robert Bathurst, Katy Wix and Mathew Horne.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Raisin
programme.desc: Parish council chairman Robert Strutters is found dead in Carsley's spring shortly before a vote over whether to allow a mineral water company access to the water. James decides to investigate with new girlfriend Mary Fortune, pushing a jealous Agatha falls into the arms of suave bottled water boss Guy Freemont. When a second body is found in the village, Agatha's competitive streak emerges and she enlists Gemma's help to find the killer before James and Mary.(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Dr Saleyha Ahsan reveals new research that could cure snoring, while Michael Mosley joins volunteers in a study to discover if changing your diet can lower cholesterol as much as drugs like statins - with surprising results. Surgeon Gabriel Weston sees the results of an unusual treatment for a hospital superbug and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigates skin care products and asks if they do more harm than good.(n)

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.sub-title: Oh Dear - Oh Dear - Oh Dear!
programme.desc: Drama about a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Guest starring Jim Carter (Downton Abbey). Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Sex Ed (2014)
programme.desc: A teacher arrives at a new school, and is appalled to discover his teenage students are receiving no sex education. He takes it upon himself to provide the facts of life, despite objection from the local preacher. However, his plan is complicated by his still being a virgin and falling in love with a pupil's older sister. Comedy, starring Haley Joel Osment and Lorenza Izzo.(n)

programme.title: Long Weekend (1978)
programme.desc: A couple take a camping holiday at a remote beach to try and rebuild their struggling relationship. Their disrespect for the wildlife and the environment provokes the wrath of a supernatural presence, and the torment they endure from it brings tensions between them to the surface. Horror, starring John Hargreaves and Briony Behets.(n)

programme.title: Essex Vendetta (2016)
programme.desc: An ordinary man is drawn into the dangerous world of organised crime as he strives to protect his family. His new life in the Essex underworld leads to him being sent on a murderous mission to Malta, where he discovers his partners in crime are not to be trusted. Crime drama, starring Michael McKell, Kris Johnson and Danniella Westbrook.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: Food busker John Quilter cooks a banh mi baguette, while cabbie Joey Bone samples three separate pie and mash shops, and a new pop-up restaurant launches in Euston.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks along the coast at Boscastle and visits Ramsey on the Isle of Man.(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: A chance to catch up on debates about the issues behind the headlines.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: The Big Money (1956)
programme.desc: An incompetent thief finally manages to make his family of crooks proud of him by pulling off a major robbery, but finds his ill-gotten gains bring him anything but happiness. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Belinda Lee, Kathleen Harrison and Robert Helpmann.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Portrait from Life (1949)
programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling.(n)

programme.title: Friend or Foe (1982)
programme.desc: Two Second World War evacuees face a dilemma when they meet a German pilot who has been shot down. Family drama, starring John Bardon and Stacey Tendeter.(n)

programme.title: The Purple Plain (1954)
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham.(n)

programme.title: The Kid From Canada (1958)
programme.desc: A boy visits his family in the Scottish Highlands, where his boastfulness gets him into trouble during a horse riding trip. Family drama, with Christopher Braden and Bernard Braden.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain.(n)

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: London-based current affair series, with Michael Hayman in conversation with Renee Elliot.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and the team recruit more than 200 volunteers to test a surprising way to cure food cravings and Dr Saleyha Ahsan shows how to spot a stroke before it happens. Dr Chris van Tulleken looks at whether organic food is good for your health, while surgeon Gabriel Weston investigates a potentially groundbreaking new treatment for cancer.(n)

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.sub-title: Mad Dog - Inside The Secret World of Muammar Gaddafi
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the private life of former Libyan dictator Colonel Muammar Muhammad Abu Minyar al-Gaddafi, revealing his disturbing abuses of power. Filmed across the Pacific, Cuba, Brazil, the US, South Africa and Australia, the programme also considers how Libya's vast oil reserves were a key reason why the 'mad dog', as President Reagan dubbed him, managed to maintain such a strong alliance with the West prior to the Arab Spring that cemented his demise at home and led to his overthrow and death in 2011.(n)

programme.title: London Real
programme.desc: Broadcaster and entrepreneur Brian Rose is in debate with some of the world's most interesting minds. Tonight he talks to National Geographic film-maker Jimmy Chin.(n)

programme.title: The Statue (1971)
programme.desc: A linguist's wife sculpts a nude statue of him to celebrate his achievements. However, when she grows weary of his neglect, she reveals that its impressive physique was modelled on another man. Convinced she has taken a lover, he sets out to identify the man behind the sculpture. Comedy, starring David Niven, Virna Lisi and Robert Vaughn.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad.(n)

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: John Quilter cooks 'posh' mushrooms on toast, taxi driver Joey Bone tries out three Italian restaurants, and one of London's few female butchers is interviewed.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
