programme.desc: Kim is brainwashed into liking hip hop, and the other girls are forced to change their music to accommodate her new obsession.(n)

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take on the Mixed Tapes on a game show, but their rivals cheat by exploiting their worst fears.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Tjure's new baby is having trouble sleeping - and cries so loudly that Halvar also gets no sleep the night before an important battle.(n)

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Snorre's mother refuses to let the harvest celebration go ahead until her smelly son has cleaned up his act - so Vic tries to trick him into washing.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: A story read by Zara makes Laurence keen to know if unicorns exist.(n)

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: Zara finds some interesting items as she hunts for dinosaur relics.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.sub-title: Tashi and the Invasion of the Buddy Snatcher
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi go in search of jade eggs, but end up breaking one, releasing a magical mist.(n)

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack stows away on a flying pirate ship to get back his stolen video games console.(n)

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: The story of the havoc caused by the V1 doodlebugs and the V2 rockets, and of the thousands of deaths that undermined the morale of a war-weary civilian population.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people.(n)

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War.(n)

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: First episode of the drama about a military bomb disposal team working in London during the blitz. New recruit Brian Ash is thrown in at the deep end. Starring Anthony Andrews.(n)

programme.title: Codebreaker
programme.sub-title: Codebreaker: The Secret Genius of World War Two
programme.desc: The first of a two-part docu-drama uncovering the secret of how a 24-year-old maths student and a GPO engineer hacked into Hitler's personal code machine - not the Enigma coding system, but something even more intimidating known as his 'secret writer'. Despite his achievements, codebreaker Alan Turing's life was dogged by tragedy.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The extraordinary stories of some of the British men and women who fought or lived through the Second World War, now in their nineties or older. The first episode looks at how national unity was tested in the 1920s and 30s with class conflict, soaring unemployment and widespread civil unrest, but despite these upheavals, those growing up in the inter-war years remember much that was positive about life in Britain at the time. When war broke out in 1939, the men and women of this generation were ready to risk their lives for their country, but the evacuation of the British Army from the beaches of Dunkirk was a wake-up call for those expecting a swift victory.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Final episode of the drama following a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Warrior Challenge
programme.desc: Action from a UCMMA event, the mixed martial arts promotion that stages bouts at The Troxy.(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Maxine Peake and Rupert Penry-Jones return in the legal drama. Clive has finally become a silk, but his celebration party is dramatically cut short when news comes through that the son of the head of chambers has been arrested for killing a police officer. Martha is assigned to defend the young man - but the evidence against him seems overwhelming. Meanwhile, Billy struggles with a secret and a new practice manager sends ripples through the clerks' room. Neil Stuke, Miranda Raison and Frances Barber co-star.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong quizzes a line-up of contestants who work on shows with a wildlife or natural history theme. They are birdwatcher Bill Oddie and historian Tessa Dunlop, Countryfile presenters John Craven and Shauna Lowry, TV gardener Diarmuid Gavin and Coast presenter Miranda Krestovnikoff, and Blue Peter hosts-turned-wildlife presenters Helen Skelton and Radzi Chinyanganya. Richard Osman is the man with the answers.(n)
